auth_notes: Requires OAuth 2.0 flow. Developers must create an application in the Podium Developer Portal to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret. n8n's built-in OAuth2 credential type can be used to manage token refreshes.
rate_limits: Standard limits are 10 requests per second (RPS) per location, with some endpoints having specific burst limits.
gotchas: Most API calls require a specific 'locationId' header or path parameter, which must be retrieved via a separate 'List Locations' call first.
Webhooks must be registered programmatically via the Subscriptions API; there is no manual webhook management UI for all event types.
The API uses specific UID formats for contacts and messages that must be mapped correctly from external systems.
research_notes: Integration will rely on the HTTP Request node. Users should configure n8n's 'OAuth2 API' credentials using Podium's authorization URL (https://api.podium.com/oauth/authorize) and token URL (https://api.podium.com/oauth/token). Webhook triggers will require a 'Webhook' node combined with an initial 'HTTP Request' to register the subscription URL with Podium.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will rely on the HTTP Request node. Users should configure n8n's 'OAuth2 API' credentials using Podium's authorization URL (https://api.podium.com/oauth/authorize) and token URL (https://api.podium.com/oauth/token).
